meta.title: Claude Opus 4.7 API | Agentic Coding, 1M Context, Vision & Dual Endpoints | APIDot
meta.description: Use Claude Opus 4.7 on APIDot for agentic coding, long-context analysis, and high-resolution vision. Access the same `claude-opus-4-7` model through `/v1/chat/completions` or `/v1/messages` for production workflows.

hero.title: Claude Opus 4.7 API for Coding, Long Context, and Vision
hero.description: Teams choose Claude Opus 4.7 when the work is too complex for a lightweight chat model, especially for repository-scale coding, structured reasoning, and screenshot or document analysis. On APIDot, the same `claude-opus-4-7` model id is available through both `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/messages`, so you can keep one production model while standardizing on the endpoint your stack already uses.

overview.title: Choose Claude Opus 4.7 when the work needs deeper engineering judgment and longer context
paragraphs: Claude Opus 4.7 makes the most sense when you are not just asking a model to chat, but asking it to inspect a codebase, reason across long documents, plan changes, or stay consistent through a multi-step task. That is where its agentic coding bias and up to 1M context matter more than a lower per-request price.

Teams usually adopt Claude Opus 4.7 because they want coding answers that feel more checked, reviews that hold together under detail, and multimodal analysis that can handle screenshots, charts, dense PDFs, and technical diagrams without collapsing on visual structure.

On APIDot, this page is built for that production decision: you use the same `claude-opus-4-7` model id, choose either `/v1/chat/completions` or `/v1/messages`, and keep pricing, playground behavior, and integration docs in one place while you standardize your stack.

capabilities.title: What Claude Opus 4.7 API is worth using for

items.title: Agentic coding for multi-step engineering work
items.description: People look for Claude Opus 4.7 when a coding task involves more than code generation, such as debugging, refactor planning, or moving through several files with consistent intent. On APIDot, you can run those workflows against the production `claude-opus-4-7` model id without changing platforms between testing and real use.

items.title: Code review, planning, and self-verification
items.description: This is the kind of model teams choose when they want reviews and plans that feel checked instead of rushed. Claude Opus 4.7 on APIDot is a better fit for structured audits, implementation plans, and higher-stakes technical reasoning than a lightweight chat model.

items.title: 1M context with up to 128K output
items.description: The long-context story matters when prompts span a repository, a contract set, or a large technical brief instead of a few paragraphs. APIDot keeps Claude Opus 4.7 positioned around a 1M token context window and up to 128K output, which is why it can cover larger analysis jobs in one pass.

items.title: High-resolution vision for screenshots, charts, and documents
items.description: Teams search for this model when visual detail actually matters, not just because it accepts images. On APIDot, Claude Opus 4.7 is a stronger option for screenshot QA, dashboard review, table reading, chart interpretation, and dense document analysis up to 2576px / 3.75MP.

items.title: Literal instruction following for production tasks
items.description: A more literal model is valuable when prompts include constraints, output formats, or review checklists that cannot be hand-waved. Claude Opus 4.7 rewards explicit instructions on APIDot, which makes it easier to standardize production prompts for engineering and knowledge workflows.

items.title: Dual endpoint support for migration and standardization
items.description: A lot of teams do not need a new model as much as they need a cleaner migration path. APIDot exposes Claude Opus 4.7 through both `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/messages`, so you can keep OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-native payloads around the same model.

useCases.title: What teams actually use Claude Opus 4.7 API for
useCases.description: This page is best suited to tasks where the prompt is long, the reasoning cannot be shallow, and the output needs to survive real engineering or knowledge-work review.
items: AI coding agents for multi-file engineering work
Repository analysis, refactor planning, and code review
Screenshot QA, dashboard review, and UI analysis
Chart, table, and dense PDF understanding
Internal copilots that need both OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-native endpoints

quickstart.title: Start using {modelName} on APIDot in three steps

steps.title: Create your API key
steps.description: Generate an APIDot API key and keep it on the server side. The same key can be used for chat, image, video, and audio models across your environment.

steps.title: Top up credits
steps.description: Claude Opus 4.7 is currently billed on APIDot at $4 per 1M input tokens and $20 per 1M output tokens. Because the model can reason deeply and produce longer completions, monitor token usage rather than assuming flat per-request cost.

steps.title: Choose the endpoint that matches your stack
steps.description: Use `/v1/chat/completions` for OpenAI-compatible chat integration, or `/v1/messages` for Anthropic-native payloads. Keep the same model id `claude-opus-4-7` in both cases.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about Claude Opus 4.7 API

items.question: What is Claude Opus 4.7 on APIDot?
items.answer: Claude Opus 4.7 on APIDot is the production `claude-opus-4-7` model for complex coding, long-context reasoning, and high-resolution multimodal work. It is a stronger fit when the task depends on careful technical judgment, longer working memory, or dense screenshot and document analysis.

items.question: Does APIDot support both `/v1/chat/completions` and `/v1/messages`?
items.answer: Yes. The same `claude-opus-4-7` model id is available through `/v1/chat/completions` for OpenAI-compatible integrations and through `/v1/messages` for Anthropic-native request payloads.

items.question: Why do teams choose Claude Opus 4.7 for coding and code review?
items.answer: Because these workflows depend on more than generating code quickly. Claude Opus 4.7 is a better match for repository analysis, refactor planning, debugging, and code review work where the model needs to stay consistent across multiple steps and show stronger self-checking behavior.

items.question: Does Claude Opus 4.7 support 1M context and up to 128K output?
items.answer: Yes. Claude Opus 4.7 is publicly positioned around a 1M token context window and up to 128K output, which is why it is a better fit for very long code, document collections, and large technical prompts than a standard chat model.

items.question: How good is Claude Opus 4.7 at screenshots, charts, and dense documents?
items.answer: This is one of the clearer reasons to use it. Claude Opus 4.7 on APIDot is better suited to screenshot QA, dashboard review, chart and table reading, and dense document analysis, with high-resolution image support up to 2576px / 3.75MP.

items.question: How much does Claude Opus 4.7 cost on APIDot?
items.answer: On APIDot, `claude-opus-4-7` is billed at $4 per 1M input tokens and $20 per 1M output tokens.

items.question: Why can real-world cost feel higher than list price?
items.answer: Claude Opus 4.7 can use more tokens than simpler models because of deeper reasoning and longer outputs. Public launch materials also note tokenizer changes, so production teams should watch prompt and completion token usage rather than budgeting only from headline pricing.

items.question: When should I choose Claude Opus 4.7 over a cheaper chat model?
items.answer: Choose it when the job depends on coding quality, repo-scale context, dense-document reading, or a more careful reasoning style. If the work is mostly lightweight chat, quick drafting, or highly cost-sensitive support flows, a smaller model will usually be the better operational choice.
